compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
Own the **remediation-mode GATE**: the freshness-window + precondition check that decides
whether a re-review runs in floored (remediation) mode or as a normal full review, and wire
it parallel to `drift_refresh` in `_run_plan_review`. (This is the task-4 candidate-check; the
Pass-3 drop math it gates is task cc5b. Body/title were previously swapped with task 3689 —
this restores the epic's 6-task decomposition.)

## Preconditions (ALL required, else byte-identical full review)

- config enabled (`remediation_mode` key; see rollout note below)
- plan changed since the prior review
- **code UNCHANGED since baseline** — detected deterministically by comparing the gate's
  attested `verified_at_sha` (the origin/main snapshot SHA the gate signs into the prior
  review's manifest) against the current run's `verified_at_sha`. Equal ⇒ code unchanged;
  differ ⇒ code drifted. This reuses the already-signed snapshot ref — no new diff machinery.
- registry unchanged (criteria-routing material fingerprint equal to the prior review's)
- a prior `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar WITH finding text is available (task e344)
- the last review of ANY kind (full or partial) is within the window (default 60 min),
  measured from that last review and **reset on each review** so the loop persists across a
  series of edits and lapses to a normal full review only after the agent goes idle.

Code-changed / both-drifted → full review (`drift_refresh` path byte-unchanged, out of scope).

## Rollout (resolves the default-on advisory)

The `remediation_mode` config key ships **default-OFF** for one release (expand-contract:
ship off → validate on the dogfood corpus → flip the default on in a follow-up), so existing
consumers do not silently get a narrowed verdict on upgrade. Setting the key off (or absent)
restores byte-identical full-review behavior at any time — the back-out.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Candidate check enforces ALL preconditions; any failure → full review (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py`, `attest.py`)
- [ ] `remediation_mode` config key (default-OFF for v1) + window minutes (default 60) in `src/rebar/config.py`; key off/absent → byte-identical full review
- [ ] "code unchanged" is the `verified_at_sha` equality check (prior manifest vs current run); registry-unchanged is the routing material-fingerprint equality check
- [ ] Window measured from the last review of any kind, reset on each review
- [ ] both-drifted → full review; `drift_refresh` path byte-unchanged
- [ ] Wiring is parallel to `drift_refresh` in `_run_plan_review`; the Pass-3 drop math itself lives in cc5b (this task only decides remediation-mode eligibility)

file_impact.reason: wire eligibility parallel to drift_refresh; record decision on coverage
latest_review_timestamp helper (window measured from last review of any kind)

compiled_state.title: Remediation-mode gate: freshness window + preconditions + wiring
